legal_notice.description: A formal pre-litigation written demand sent to the opposite party before filing a case.
legal_notice.send_via: Registered Post with Acknowledgement Due (RPAD) + email

fir_draft.description: First Information Report drafted for lodging with police. FIR initiates criminal proceedings.
fir_draft.send_via: In person at police station; online on state police portal
fir_draft.response_time: Police must register FIR immediately for cognizable offences

consumer_complaint.description: Formal complaint to District Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (DCDRC) for goods or service deficiency.
consumer_complaint.send_via: In person at DCDRC / e-daakhil.gov.in portal
consumer_complaint.response_time: Opposite party gets 30 days to reply; order within 150 days

police_complaint.description: Written complaint to police that may or may not result in FIR. For non-cognizable offences.
police_complaint.send_via: In person at police station or online state portal

affidavit.description: A sworn statement of facts made before a Magistrate or Notary. Used as evidence in court or for official purposes.
affidavit.send_via: Notarized on stamp paper (₹10–₹100 depending on state)

cyber_complaint.description: Online complaint at cybercrime.gov.in or 1930 helpline for internet/digital fraud.
cyber_complaint.send_via: cybercrime.gov.in or Cyber Crime police station

labour_court_application.description: Formal application to Labour Court or Payment of Wages Authority for recovery of wages.
labour_court_application.send_via: Labour Court in district of workplace

national.eligibility: Women, SC/ST, children, persons with disability, victims of trafficking, persons with annual income below ₹3 lakh

state.authority: SLSA — State Legal Services Authority (each state has one)
state.note: Visit your District Legal Services Authority (DLSA) at your district court complex
